action.reasoning: Observation: 2-epoch CLIP fine-tuning completed but val macro-F1 is essentially zero (0.0034), indicating the current setup is not learning useful class separation. The immediate failure was only in the local predict smoke test because /test_data/test.txt was unavailable; training/checkpointing worked. Analysis: full CLIP end-to-end fine-tuning at this scale is too slow (~1.7h for 2 epochs) and the current objective is poor for long-tail OOD generalization. A much stronger and faster baseline is frozen CLIP feature extraction plus a balanced linear classifier trained on all train features, with optional test-time ensembling over center + flip. This should drastically reduce iteration time and improve macro-F1 on rare classes via class-balanced loss/weights. Also predict.py should support fallback to val.txt for local smoke tests. Decision: rewrite model/train/predict around frozen CLIP embeddings + linear head, train the head for a few epochs, validate locally on val split, and submit eval immediately.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 8 --batch_size 128 --lr 3e-3 --num_workers 8 --save_path checkpoints/best.pt && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output val_preds.json
